focus: traceability: REQ-006..011 spec alignment, CHK-070..078 evidence, continuity frontmatter, feature-catalog overlay, and FIX ADDENDUM verification commands

findingDetails.title: LeaseId checks do not fence heartbeat replacement or cleanup against a successor interleaving
Continuity frontmatter uses contradictory phase labels
FIX ADDENDUM verification column is not uniformly re-runnable

traceabilityChecks.spec_code: passed for REQ-006 through REQ-009 and REQ-011; REQ-010 complete-fencing claim remains bounded by existing P1-001
traceabilityChecks.checklist_evidence: passed spot checks for CHK-070, CHK-072, CHK-073, CHK-074 scope, and CHK-076; CHK-079 correctly deferred
traceabilityChecks.continuity_frontmatter: P2 inconsistency in plan.md phase labels
traceabilityChecks.feature_catalog_code: notApplicable: no product feature entry for internal daemon hardening
traceabilityChecks.affected_surfaces: P2: two verification cells are manual or underspecified

claim: REQ-010 and CHK-074 overstate complete lease fencing because refresh/clear validate leaseId before a later mutation without atomic binding
counterevidenceSought: The cited interleaving test covers stale reclaim but does not exercise heartbeat refresh or cleanup successor interleavings
alternativeExplanation: The post-write reread detects some races after mutation, but cannot undo a successor overwrite or unlink
downgradeTrigger: A deterministic test or atomic mutation primitive demonstrates refresh and cleanup are bound to the validated leaseId

claim: plan.md continuity labels contradict the other four packet documents
counterevidenceSought: Check whether Phase 5/Phase 7 are intentionally separate numbering schemes rather than stale continuity text
alternativeExplanation: The plan may preserve original numbering while later docs use execution numbering, but the shared next-action fields do not explain that distinction
downgradeTrigger: A packet-level phase-numbering convention explicitly defines both labels and their intended use

claim: The affected-surfaces verification table contains non-deterministic or underspecified verification cells
counterevidenceSought: Attempt to execute every table cell as written and verify that each names a concrete command/test
alternativeExplanation: The table may be a planning aid where manual timing and aggregate commands are intentionally shorthand
downgradeTrigger: The table is explicitly declared non-executable shorthand or the two cells are replaced with exact commands/test paths
